svg.style: font-family:Lato, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, Arial, 'Liberation Sans', sans-serif;font-feature-settings:"liga", "kern", "calt", "lnum";text-rendering:geometricPrecision;-webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased;font-size:18px;background-color:#ffffff

a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan: Labor force participation rates: modeled vs. national estimates

Share of the working-age population who are economically active (employed or unemployed).
National

estimates
are based on each country's own data collection and definitions, while
modeled estimates
are
produced by the International Labour Organization (ILO) using statistical methods to improve comparability
Data source: Labour Force Statistics, via World Bank (2026); ILO Modelled Estimates, via World Bank (2026)
Note: Employment is defined in line with the 13th ICLS and includes the production of goods for own use, such as subsistence farming.
